chunks.text:
# Business Glossary - E-Commerce Domain (Simplified)

This document defines the core business concepts for the e-commerce platform test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase.

**Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, an... [TRUNCATED]

**Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.

**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not chan... [TRUNCATED]

Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product

Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order

Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order
- A Shipment co... [TRUNCATED]

## MODIFIED FOR TEST A
Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

## SECOND MODIFICATION — extra term
Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

# Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-----------... [TRUNCATED]

**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)

**Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order.

**Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| LINE_ID | INT | Unique line identifier | NO... [TRUNCATED]

CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID

TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID

TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference)

SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

BUSINESS CONCEPT TO TABLE MAPPING
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder     ... [TRUNCATED]

entities_post_resolution.name: Active/Inactive Status
entities_post_resolution.definition: A binary indicator that specifies whether a record or item is currently active or inactive within the business system.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Active/inactive status | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Order ID
- References exactly one Customer
- Order date when transaction was initiated
- Total monetary value
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Master
entities_post_resolution.definition: A master data entity representing unique customers, identified by CUST_ID, used as the source of truth for customer information and referenced by related tables such as sales orders.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER | - CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | **Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Customer is an individual or entity registered on the e-commerce platform who has made at least one purchase.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase. | **Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, and receive shipments. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Customer ID
- Valid email address (required for login) | **Key Attributes**:
- One or more delivery/billing addresses | **Key Attributes**:
- Geographic region for tax calculation | **Key Attributes**:
- Active/inactive status | **Business Rules**:
- Must place at least one order to remain active | **Business Rules**:
- Can have multiple addresses but only one primary per type | **Business Rules**:
- Data retained for 7 years after last purchase | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | **Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers. | | CUST_ID | INT | Unique customer identifier | NO | | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER | Refund: A reimbursement issued

entities_post_resolution.name: Line Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: A line item is an individual product entry on a sales order that records the product reference, quantity, unit price at the time of ordering, and the extended amount, and is associated with the order.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | **Synonyms**: line item, order detail, order row | **Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not change with future price updates

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Line Item.Product Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: Represents the product identifier associated with a single line item within a sales order.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID | **Description**: Header record for each customer sales order. | | ORDER_ID | BIGINT | Unique order identifier | NO | | - ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | **Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | **Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order. | **Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment) | OrderLineItem    → ORDER_LINE_ITEM | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique Order ID
- References exactly one Customer
- Order date when transaction was initiated
- Total monetary value
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED
- Must contain at least one Order Line Item | **Definition**: A single line within a Sales Order specifying a Product, quantity, and unit price at time of purchase. | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical,

entities_post_resolution.name: Payment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Payment is a monetary transaction record used to settle a Sales Order, including a unique Payment ID, amount, method, timestamp, status, and a link to exactly one Sales Order (with the possibility of multiple payments for a single order).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unique Payment ID
- References exactly one Sales Order
- Payment method (credit card, PayPal, bank transfer)
- Payment amount
- Confirmation timestamp
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, FAILED, REFUNDED | **Business Rules**:
- Payment must be confirmed before order ships
- Refunds create new payment records with negative amounts
- Multiple payment attempts can exist for one order | **Payment Relationships**:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | Payment          → PAYMENT | **Description**: Monetary transactions settling order amounts. | **Primary Key**: PAYMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: The product entity represents an individual sellable item in the e-commerce catalog, identified by a unique SKU and a human-readable name.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unique SKU code | **Key Attributes**:
- Human-readable product name | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price in base currency (EUR) | **Key Attributes**:
- Active/inactive status | **Key Attributes**:
- Belongs to exactly one Category | **Business Rules**:
- Must belong to a valid category before being sold | **Business Rules**:
- Prices can change daily based on market conditions | **Business Rules**:
- Inactive products cannot be ordered but remain for historical reference | **Synonyms**: item, article, goods, merchandise, SKU | **Related Concepts**: Category, Inventory, OrderLineItem | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | **Order Line Item Relationships**:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product | **Product Relationships**:
- A Produc

entities_post_resolution.name: Sales Order Hdr
entities_post_resolution.definition: Sales order header record representing a unique sales order, identified by ORDER_ID and associated with a customer via CUST_ID.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
SalesOrder       → SALES_ORDER_HDR | - ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | **Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A shipment is the unit of physical delivery of goods from a warehouse to a customer, identified by a unique shipment ID, associated with a sales order, carrying shipment details (source, destination, timestamps, tracking number) and a lifecycle status (e.g., PREPARING, SHIPPED, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- Unique Shipment ID
- References exactly one Sales Order
- Source Warehouse
- Destination Customer Address
- Shipped timestamp
- Tracking number
- Status: PREPARING, SHIPPED, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED | **Business Rules**:
- Can only be created for confirmed orders with payment
- One order may have multiple partial shipments
- Delivery address must belong to the order's customer | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment comes from exactly one Warehouse | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment goes to exactly one Customer Address | Shipment         → SHIPMENT | **Description**: Physical delivery of products to customers. | **Primary Key**: SHIPMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | **Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, and receive shipments. | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

entities_post_resolution.name: Tb Category.Parent Category Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: TB_PRODUCT(PRODUCT_ID) represents the product master data entity/table identified by PRODUCT_ID, used in business processes such as orders and product-category associations.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference) | TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference) | TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID | Category         → TB_CATEGORY | TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID | Product          → TB_PRODUCT | - PRODUCT_ID → TB_PRODUCT(PRODUCT_ID) | TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID

entities_post_resolution.name: Multiple Addresses
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Business Rules**:
- Can have multiple addresses but only one primary per type
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- Can have multiple addresses but only one primary per type

entities_post_resolution.name: Not Yet Shipped
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Business Rules**:
- Can only be cancelled if not yet shipped
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- Can only be cancelled if not yet shipped

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Detail
entities_post_resolution.definition: **Synonyms**: line item, order detail, order row
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: **Synonyms**: line item, order detail, order row

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Row
entities_post_resolution.definition: **Synonyms**: line item, order detail, order row
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: **Synonyms**: line item, order detail, order row

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Ships
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Business Rules**:
- Payment must be confirmed before order ships
- Refunds create new payment records with negative amounts
- Multiple payment attempts can exist for one order
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- Payment must be confirmed before order ships
- Refunds create new payment records with negative amounts
- Multiple payment attempts can exist for one order

entities_post_resolution.name: Ordered
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Business Rules**:
- Inactive products cannot be ordered but remain for historical reference
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- Inactive products cannot be ordered but remain for historical reference

entities_post_resolution.name: Moment Of Ordering
entities_post_resolution.definition: **Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: **Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.

entities_post_resolution.name: Transaction Document
entities_post_resolution.definition: **Definition**: A formal transaction document recording the agreement between the platform and a Customer to supply Products.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: **Definition**: A formal transaction document recording the agreement between the platform and a Customer to supply Products.

entities_post_resolution.name: Valid Category
entities_post_resolution.definition:
**Business Rules**:
- Must belong to a valid category before being sold
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Business Rules**:
- Must belong to a valid category before being sold

tables_enriched.name: Customer Master
tables_enriched.description: Stores core customer information including identity, contact details, region, account creation timestamp, and active status.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products, including identifiers, SKU, name, category reference, price, and whether the product is currently active.

tables_enriched.name: Category
tables_enriched.description: This table stores the categories and their hierarchical relationships for products.

tables_enriched.name: Sales Order Header
tables_enriched.description: Stores the header information for sales orders, including customer reference, order date, total amount, status, payment confirmation time, and delivery/creation timestamps.

tables_enriched.name: Order Line Item
tables_enriched.description: Stores the line items for each order, including the product, quantity, unit price, and total line amount for that item.

tables_enriched.name: Payment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of payments associated with customer orders, including the method, amount, status, and timestamps for confirmation and creation.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of shipments linked to customer orders, including where they are shipped from, when they were sent, tracking details, and current shipment status.
